messages.il:
The attack this evening on March 29.

Graphic images of the dead are circulating online. If they reached you, do yourselves and the families a favor and delete them. Stop the chain of distribution.

Palestinian sources: the attacker, Diaa Hamarsheh, was an operative in the military wing of Islamic Jihad, from the Jenin Brigade.
(A released prisoner from the village of Ya'bad, Jenin district)
messages.ps:
#Urgent| Injuries, some of them critical, in a shooting in the Bnei Brak area in the occupied interior

#Urgent| Hebrew media: the number of those killed in the shooting operation in Tel Aviv has risen to 5

Urgent | Israeli official television Channel 11: the attacker is a Palestinian from the village of Ya'bad near Jenin in the northern West Bank
messages.neutral: On March 29, 2022, a shooting in Bnei Brak was reported to have caused casualties and injuries, with later reports saying five people were killed; reports identified the suspected attacker as a Palestinian from Ya‘bad near Jenin and linked him to Islamic Jihad.

messages.il:
The shooting in Tel Aviv

Unofficial media outlets identified with Islamic Jihad note that this evening's attack in Tel Aviv began at 21:00, the hour associated with Baha' Abu al-Ata, the senior Islamic Jihad figure who was killed in Gaza by the IDF in 2019.

Gaza

The mosques in Gaza are now praising the attack in Tel Aviv with cries of: Allahu Akbar.
messages.ps:
#Urgent| Hebrew Channel 12: the latest toll from the shooting operation in Tel Aviv: two dead and 15 wounded

#Urgent| Islamic Jihad Movement: the Tel Aviv operation is a clear message to the occupation that it must stop its incursions into Al-Aqsa Mosque
messages.neutral: On April 7, 2022, a shooting in Tel Aviv was reported to have killed two people and injured others; some reports and statements framed the timing and motive in relation to events around Al-Aqsa, and reactions were reported in Gaza.

messages.il:
A senior Al Jazeera correspondent was killed in an exchange of fire in the Jenin refugee camp
For the first time in three weeks, IDF fighters entered the stronghold of the terror organizations in the northern West Bank. During the shooting, the veteran journalist Shireen Abu Akleh, who has been covering the area for the past two decades, was hit.
messages.ps:
Shireen Abu Akleh: Al Jazeera correspondent killed by Israeli forces' bullets
Israeli forces shot Abu Akleh in the head while she was carrying out a journalistic assignment in Jenin in the occupied West Bank.
messages.neutral: Shireen Abu Akleh, an Al Jazeera journalist, was shot and killed while reporting in Jenin in the West Bank during an Israeli operation.

messages.il:
Israeli flags were torn down, Palestinian flags were confiscated: clashes in Jerusalem at the journalist's funeral
Security forces clashed with participants in the funeral procession, and footage from the scene showed the coffin almost falling. Police: "Lawbreakers damaged the funeral procession and threw stones from near the coffin, 6 were arrested"
messages.ps:
Photos: Israeli forces attack Shireen Abu Akleh's funeral

Israeli police stormed the area around one of Jerusalem's hospitals to prevent the funeral procession of Shireen Abu Akleh by force. She was the Al Jazeera journalist who was killed by Israeli forces' bullets while covering an Israeli army raid in Jenin on Wednesday.
messages.neutral: During Shireen Abu Akleh’s funeral procession in Jerusalem, clashes broke out between Israeli police and participants, and video from the scene showed the coffin nearly falling as officers moved through the crowd.

messages.il:
The Knesset was finally dissolved: elections on November 1, Lapid will become the 14th prime minister at midnight
In another 124 days we are going to the polls again: the dissolution of the Knesset was finally approved, and the chairman of Yesh Atid will enter the role of transitional prime minister at midnight. Bennett swapped places with him: "I hand you the baton, this country belongs to no one."
messages.ps:
Israel.. dissolving the Knesset for early elections in November
Ninety-two out of 120 members voted on Thursday in favor of dissolving parliament, in a vote that will also allow Foreign Minister Yair Lapid to assume the premiership, succeeding Naftali Bennett
messages.neutral: Israel’s parliament voted to dissolve the Knesset, triggering early elections in November and leading to a transition in which Foreign Minister Yair Lapid was set to assume the role of interim prime minister.

messages.il: The IDF launched Operation "Breaking Dawn" against Islamic Jihad in Gaza; senior figures in the organization were eliminated
messages.ps:
Gaza.. Islamic Jihad launches 100 rockets at Israel and the occupation announces the continuation of its operation and calls up reserve forces
Dozens of martyrs and wounded in heavy strikes on Gaza.. Islamic Jihad shells Tel Aviv and Ben Gurion Airport
messages.neutral: Israel launched Operation “Breaking Dawn” in Gaza against Palestinian Islamic Jihad, while the group fired rockets toward Israel and Israel carried out airstrikes and mobilized reserve forces amid reported casualties.

messages.il: Israel confirms and warns Islamic Jihad: a ceasefire starting at 23:30
messages.ps: Israel and the "Jihad Movement" had announced their acceptance of an Egyptian call for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, ending an escalation between the two sides that caused dozens of victims in the Strip since Friday.
messages.neutral: Israel and Palestinian Islamic Jihad agreed to an Egyptian-brokered ceasefire in Gaza scheduled to begin at 23:30, ending several days of escalation.

messages.il:
The "arch-terrorist" who fired at military positions: wanted man Ibrahim al-Nabulsi has been eliminated
The terrorist, who had been a member of a cell eliminated in February and had since escaped several times, was shot dead in an exchange of fire with Yamam, Shin Bet, and IDF personnel in Nablus, after he refused to turn himself in.
messages.ps: #Urgent| Hebrew sources: the occupation police raise the level of alert in the occupied West Bank after the assassination of the wanted man Ibrahim al-Nabulsi
messages.neutral: Ibrahim al-Nabulsi was killed in Nablus during an Israeli security operation that involved an exchange of fire, after authorities had been seeking his arrest.

messages.il: Jamal Majdoub, the terrorist who carried out the shooting attack yesterday in Hawara and was later eliminated by an IDF force, arrived, according to Palestinian sources, together with his brother from Jordan and settled in his grandmother's house in the village of Far'un in the Tulkarm district.
messages.ps: Hamas Movement: we mourn the martyr mujahid Jamal Majdoub (23 years old) from the town of Far'un, Tulkarm district, who was martyred by the occupation forces' fire after carrying out a heroic shooting operation in Hawara, and the heroic martyr Labib Mohammad Labib Dmeidi (19 years old), who rose by the bullets of criminal settlers...
messages.neutral: Israeli and Palestinian sources reported that Jamal Majdoub from the Tulkarm area carried out a shooting in Hawara and was later killed by Israeli forces.

messages.il:
Arab documentation:

Gunfire at our forces right now in the village of Burqa, Nablus district.
No casualties were reported.
messages.ps: Local sources: "Gunfire targets the occupation forces during their raid into the village of Burqa northwest of Nablus."
messages.neutral: Gunfire was reported toward Israeli forces during an operation in Burqa, northwest of Nablus. No injuries were reported.

messages.il:
Northern Samaria:

About 40 residents of Kibbutz Merav entered on foot this morning into the Palestinian village of Jilbun in the Jenin district.

This followed incidents of gunfire by terrorists toward the kibbutz with no practical solution from the IDF.
The residents of the kibbutz declared that they intend to organize and respond to these shooting incidents if a solution is not given soon.
messages.ps:
⭕️ Settlers storm the town of Jilbun east of Jenin from the southern side a short while ago.

N
 #Ahrar_Tulkarm🔥🖤
messages.neutral: Reports said a group of residents from Kibbutz Merav entered the Palestinian village of Jilbun near Jenin on foot, following earlier reports of gunfire toward the kibbutz.

messages.il: As of now, 859 Jewish worshippers have gone up to the Temple Mount plaza (today, Sunday) during Sukkot.
messages.ps: Hebrew media published a video documenting settlers bringing symbols of the so-called Feast of Tabernacles (the etrog, willow, palm frond, and myrtle) into Al-Aqsa Mosque.
messages.neutral: During Sukkot, visitors brought ritual plant items (the ‘four species’) to the Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa compound, drawing criticism in Palestinian coverage and reporting in Israeli coverage.

messages.il:
Palestinian channels report: "Settlers carrying plant offerings in their hands are reciting Talmudic verses and asking to enter Al-Aqsa"

The Palestinians call the "four species of Sukkot" by the name "plant sacrifices" in order to draw them terminologically closer to "animal sacrifices"—as a renewal of the custom as part of worship on the Temple Mount—something that helps fuel incitement around Al-Aqsa. According to the Palestinians, the Jews brought the "plant sacrifices" into Al-Aqsa as an offering to God.

—
Sukkot is one of the three pilgrimage festivals ("regalim") in Judaism. During the Temple period, adult men who were able were required to visit and bring their offerings to the Temple in Jerusalem.
messages.ps: The Hebrew newspaper "Maariv": the occupation police are making preparations and reinforcing their forces in Jerusalem to secure the so-called Priestly Blessing at the al-Buraq Wall in Al-Aqsa Mosque during the "Feast of Tabernacles," in which thousands of settlers are expected to take part, and which will be held today Monday and Wednesday in the morning hours.
messages.neutral: During Sukkot, visitors brought ritual plant items (the ‘four species’) to the Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa compound, drawing criticism in Palestinian coverage and reporting in Israeli coverage.

messages.il:
Jenin refugee camp: exchange of fire and the throwing of explosive devices.
Unofficial Palestinian reports of an armed Palestinian killed by IDF fire.
messages.ps: Occupied Palestine: clashes between resistance fighters and occupation forces in Jenin camp in the northern West Bank, and the occupation is preventing ambulance crews from entering it
messages.neutral: Clashes and an exchange of gunfire were reported in the Jenin refugee camp during an Israeli military operation, with reports that one Palestinian was killed.

messages.il: The terror organizations cast doubt on the Palestinian Authority and stated that the continued detention of the terrorist Musab Ashtiyeh undermines the credibility of the Authority's decision to stop security coordination with Israel.
messages.ps: 🚨 The treacherous Palestinian Authority claims that it has, so far, ended all forms of "security coordination" with the Zionist enemy. Yet the fact should not be ignored that the Palestinian Authority had prior knowledge three days in advance of the raid launched today on Jenin, which resulted in the martyrdom of ten people. It remains to be seen whether the Authority will keep its promise or continue its policy of selling the resistance, the land, and the cause to the occupation.
messages.neutral: Reports discuss Palestinian Authority statements about ending security coordination with Israel, alongside criticism questioning the credibility of that claim, including references to the continued detention of Musab Ashtiyeh and alleged prior knowledge of an Israeli raid in Jenin.

messages.il: The Palestinians in the Jenin refugee camp: we succeeded in bringing down an IDF drone by gunfire this morning.
messages.ps: The moment resistance fighters shot down an Israeli reconnaissance drone during the clashes with the occupation army in Jenin camp this morning.
messages.neutral: Palestinian sources in Jenin said an Israeli drone was shot down during clashes in the camp.

messages.il: Askar refugee camp in Nablus: signs of joy following the attack in Jerusalem.
messages.ps: Fireworks launched in Nablus after seven settlers were killed in the Jerusalem operation.
messages.neutral: Fireworks were set off in Nablus following a deadly incident in Jerusalem in which seven Israelis were killed.

messages.il: A shooting attack at this hour in the City of David, in East Jerusalem. According to Israeli media there are two wounded and the terrorist was neutralized. Here in the attached video, a Palestinian documents the attack from afar and calls out: "Allahu Akbar."
messages.ps: #Urgent| Two settlers wounded, one of them seriously, in a shooting operation in Silwan in occupied Jerusalem.
messages.neutral: Israeli and Palestinian media reported a shooting in the Silwan/City of David area of East Jerusalem, with two people reported injured. Israeli outlets said the suspected attacker was neutralized.

messages.il: A shooting attack at this hour in the City of David, in East Jerusalem. According to Israeli media there are two wounded and the terrorist was neutralized. Here in the attached video, a Palestinian documents the attack from afar and calls out: "Allahu Akbar."
messages.ps: Occupation forces on alert in the town of Silwan in Jerusalem after settlers were wounded in a shooting operation
messages.neutral: Reports said Israeli forces were deployed in Silwan in East Jerusalem following a shooting in which two people were reported injured. Israeli media said the suspected attacker was neutralized.

messages.il: In Nablus and Tulkarm they are distributing sweets following the attack this morning in Jerusalem.
messages.ps: Press coverage: "Distributing sweets in the Old City of Nablus in celebration of the heroic operation in Silwan in Jerusalem."
messages.neutral: Reports said sweets were distributed in parts of Nablus and Tulkarm after a shooting incident in Silwan in East Jerusalem earlier that day.

messages.il: Heavy fire from Gaza: sirens were heard in the Dan Bloc and in the Gaza border communities.
messages.ps: Urgent | Al-Qassam commander-in-chief Mohammed Deif: we announce the start of Operation Al-Aqsa Flood, and we announce that the first strike, which targeted enemy sites, its airports, and its military fortifications, exceeded 5,000 rockets and shells.
messages.neutral: Heavy rocket fire from Gaza triggered sirens in central Israel and near the Gaza border, while Hamas announced the start of ‘Al-Aqsa Flood’ and said the first strike involved thousands of rockets and projectiles.

messages.il: The IDF declares a state of war alert: terrorists infiltrated Israel at several points.
messages.ps: Initial photos of elite Qassam forces inside our occupied lands as part of the Battle of Al-Aqsa Flood.
messages.neutral: Israel’s military said armed infiltrations occurred at multiple locations, while Qassam sources shared images they said showed their fighters operating inside Israel during the Oct. 7 attack.

messages.il: Armed terrorists inside Sderot, a battle at the police station.
messages.ps: Hebrew media: 10 dead in front of the gate of the Sderot police station, all of them police, and Hamas elements fully control the station
messages.neutral: Reports described armed attackers entering Sderot and fighting at the city’s police station, with claims of multiple fatalities and that the station was overtaken during the clashes.

messages.il: A Be'eri kibbutz resident in tears: terrorists are trying to break into my house, why is there no help?
messages.ps:
⭕️ Channel 13 correspondent: the battle in the settlement of Be'eri and Kfar Aza is ongoing with resistance gunmen; control has not yet been fully secured.
q
#Ahrar_Tulkarm🔥🖤
messages.neutral: A resident of Kibbutz Be’eri reported armed people attempting to break into homes, while media reports said fighting was still ongoing in Be’eri and Kfar Aza and control had not yet been fully restored.

messages.il: At least 10 terrorists were hiding in Kfar Aza; significant exchanges of fire in the kibbutz.
messages.ps: Hebrew media: the clashes in Kfar Aza continue, and settlement residents are talking about a nightmare that has come true.
messages.neutral: Heavy clashes continued in Kfar Aza, with Israeli messaging claiming armed individuals remained in the area and Palestinian messaging describing the situation as severe for residents.

messages.il: A direct hit on a building in Tel Aviv: the last barrage caused heavy damage.
messages.ps: In response to the destruction of a residential tower... Al-Qassam shells Tel Aviv with 130 rockets.
messages.neutral: A rocket barrage toward the Tel Aviv area was reported to have struck a building and caused major damage, while Hamas’s Qassam Brigades said they fired 130 rockets.

messages.il: IDF Spokesperson: hundreds of terrorists have been eliminated in the Gaza border area and in the south.
messages.ps: Urgent | Gaza Health Ministry: the toll from the Israeli aggression on the Gaza Strip has risen to 370 martyrs and 2,200 wounded.
messages.neutral: Israel’s military said it killed hundreds of militants in southern Israel and the Gaza border area, while Gaza’s health authorities reported 370 people killed and 2,200 injured in Gaza during Israeli strikes.

messages.il: At least 10 terrorists were hiding in Kfar Aza; significant exchanges of fire in the kibbutz.
messages.ps: Urgent | Shehab correspondent: very violent clashes between Palestinian fighters and occupation forces in the settlement of Be'eri, and helicopters are firing intensively.
messages.neutral: Reports described continued intense fighting in Israeli border communities, including claims of armed individuals still present in Kfar Aza and accounts of heavy clashes in Be’eri with helicopter fire during the operation.

messages.il: Netanyahu: I offered Gantz and Lapid entry into an emergency government, like on the eve of the Six-Day War.
messages.ps: Hebrew media: Israeli opposition leader Lapid refuses to join an emergency government headed by Netanyahu.
messages.neutral: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he invited opposition leaders Benny Gantz and Yair Lapid to join an emergency government, while reports said Lapid declined to participate.

messages.il: The first stage of the hostage deal has begun, during which 13 women and children held captive by Hamas in the Strip were released. They were handed over to the Red Cross in Khan Younis and arrived in Egypt, from where they will be transferred to Israel. In accordance with the agreement, 39 Palestinian prisoners will be released from Ofer Prison in the coming hours. Members of Kibbutz Nir Oz identified two of the abductees
messages.ps: On Friday evening, the Israeli occupation forces released 39 male and female prisoners, women and children under the age of 19, in exchange for the Qassam Brigades releasing 13 Israeli prisoners it held.
messages.neutral: In the first phase of a prisoner-and-hostage exchange, 13 Israeli women and children held in Gaza were transferred to the Red Cross and moved via Egypt, while Israel released 39 Palestinian women and minors from detention.

messages.il: More than 1,590 wounded, among them 312 seriously and critically. The assessments: about 100 people, including civilians and soldiers, were abducted.
messages.ps: The Izz al-Din al-Qassam Brigades (the military wing of Hamas) announced today, Saturday, that they had captured a number of occupation soldiers during the Battle of Al-Aqsa Flood. Al-Qassam broadcast footage of the capture of some soldiers from inside the Gaza border settlements, after taking control of Israeli military sites this morning.
messages.neutral: Israeli reports said more than 1,590 people were injured and estimated about 100 people were abducted, while Hamas’s Qassam Brigades said they captured several Israeli soldiers and released footage they said showed those captures.

messages.il: A day after the strike on the hospital in Gaza, where according to Palestinians hundreds of people were killed, the army continues its public-relations effort to prove to the world that it is not responsible. The IDF Spokesperson revealed footage and evidence: "There are no craters, this was not an aerial bombing. Hamas knew that Islamic Jihad was responsible and launched a campaign." The terrorists were heard in the recording: "It's Islamic Jihad's."
messages.ps: A horrific massacre.. 500 martyrs in the occupation aircraft's bombing of the Baptist Hospital in Gaza
messages.neutral: After an explosion at Al-Ahli Hospital in Gaza that Palestinian sources said killed about 500 people, the Israeli military denied responsibility and said the blast was caused by a rocket launched from Gaza that fell short.

messages.il: IDF fighter jets, guided by Shin Bet intelligence, eliminated Ibrahim Biari, commander of Hamas's central Jabalia battalion • Biari was among the leaders of the murderous terrorist attack on October 7 and was involved in the 2004 attack at Ashdod port in which 13 Israelis were murdered
messages.ps: Hundreds of martyrs and wounded in a horrific Israeli massacre in Jabalia camp
messages.neutral: Israel said it killed Ibrahim Biari, described as a Hamas commander in Jabalia, in an airstrike, while Palestinian coverage said the strike in Jabalia caused large numbers of deaths and injuries.

messages.il: Dramatic IDF raid on Shifa Hospital: 'Explosive devices at the entrance, terrorists eliminated'
messages.ps: The aggression on Gaza enters its 40th day and occupation forces storm the al-Shifa medical complex
messages.neutral: Israeli forces entered the al-Shifa medical complex in Gaza amid ongoing fighting, with Israel saying it found explosives and killed armed fighters.

messages.il: 39 Palestinian terrorists were released in the third round of the hostage deal, among them youths who possessed weapons, carried out shooting at civilians, tried to carry out attacks using explosive devices, committed arson on a nationalist motive, and even caused injuries. One of the prisoners was released to Rafah
messages.ps: The Israeli Prison Service received a list of the names of 39 Palestinian prisoners scheduled to be released today, while the occupation expects the return of 13 Israeli prisoners from the Gaza Strip as part of the truce agreement with the Palestinian resistance.
messages.neutral: As part of a truce-linked exchange, Israel released 39 Palestinian prisoners, while 13 Israeli captives were returned from Gaza.

messages.il: The hostage list is still being examined: it is problematic, there are intensive negotiations
messages.ps: U.S. National Security Council strategic communications coordinator John Kirby announced today, Monday, that President Joe Biden agreed with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to extend the pause in fighting in Gaza and the period for releasing Israeli prisoners held by the resistance.
messages.neutral: Negotiations continued over extending the pause in fighting in Gaza and the timetable for releases, as Israel said the hostage list was still under review and U.S. officials said an extension had been agreed in principle.

messages.il: Two terrorists, brothers from the Sur Baher neighborhood, opened fire at the bus stop at the exit from Jerusalem and murdered in cold blood 3 Jews, a man and two women, who were waiting at the station
messages.ps: Al-Qassam claims responsibility for the Jerusalem operation: a response to the occupation's crimes in the West Bank and Gaza
messages.neutral: A shooting attack near Jerusalem killed three people, and Hamas's armed wing said it was responsible.

messages.il: Around 7 in the morning Hamas began firing rockets toward the border communities. The IDF attacked in response, and later the Air Force launched another wave of strikes.
messages.ps: More than 100 martyrs.. with the end of the truce and the renewal of the aggression on the Gaza Strip
messages.neutral: After the truce ended, Hamas fired rockets toward southern Israel and Israel carried out airstrikes in Gaza, with Palestinian sources reporting more than 100 people killed.

messages.il: As part of the discussion, the Assembly approved a nonbinding draft resolution calling for an immediate humanitarian ceasefire and the immediate release of all the hostages, without condemning Hamas.
messages.ps: A U.S. veto brings down a draft resolution in the Security Council calling for a ceasefire in Gaza
messages.neutral: Diplomatic efforts at the UN included a U.S. veto of a Security Council draft calling for a Gaza ceasefire and a separate, nonbinding General Assembly resolution urging an immediate humanitarian ceasefire and the release of all hostages.

messages.il: 3 hostages were mistakenly shot by the IDF in Gaza and killed
messages.ps: After the occupation killed its prisoners in Gaza.. demonstrations in "Tel Aviv" demand the return of the detainees
messages.neutral: Israel said its forces in Gaza mistakenly shot and killed three Israeli hostages, prompting demonstrations in Tel Aviv calling for the return of remaining captives.

messages.il: 3 terrorists were eliminated in an unusual raid on the hospital in Jenin: they used it as a human shield
messages.ps: An Israeli force assassinates 3 Palestinian youths, one of them wounded, inside a hospital in Jenin
messages.neutral: Israeli forces carried out an operation at a hospital in Jenin in which three Palestinians were killed.

messages.il: An ambulance was attacked in Gaza; the IDF: we eliminated from the air a Hamas cell that was hiding in it
messages.ps: The United Nations condemns the shelling of ambulances in Gaza and demands an end to the killing and besieging of civilians
messages.neutral: An ambulance in Gaza was hit during ongoing fighting.

messages.il: The terrorist who held about a thousand Gaza residents as hostages was eliminated. Siam was eliminated while hiding in al-Buraq School, where several Hamas terrorists under his command were also hiding and were eliminated together with him.
messages.ps: 50 martyrs inside al-Buraq School in Gaza after missile and artillery shelling
messages.neutral: Dozens of people were reported killed at Al-Buraq School in Gaza amid ongoing hostilities, with Israeli sources saying a Hamas commander was targeted and Palestinian sources reporting deaths at the school following strikes.

messages.il: A death trap in a traffic jam: a young man was murdered and 6 were injured in an attack on Route 1 near Ma'ale Adumim
messages.ps: Details of the "Ma'ale Adumim" operation revealed: Hebrew media reported that estimates by the occupation police indicate that the shooting operation near the settlement of "Ma'ale Adumim," built on the lands of the towns of al-Eizariya and Abu Dis east of occupied Jerusalem, was planned in advance.
messages.neutral: One person was killed and six were injured in a shooting attack on Route 1 near Ma'ale Adumim, east of Jerusalem.

messages.il: A shooting attack at a gas station near Eli: a man and a young man were murdered, the terrorist was eliminated
messages.ps: Hebrew sources revealed a shooting at the fuel station in the settlement of "Eli" south of Nablus this Thursday evening, 29 February, which led to the killing of Israeli settlers.
messages.neutral: Reports said two Israelis were killed in a shooting at a gas station near Eli.

messages.il: No unusual incidents: about 40 thousand at the prayers of the first Friday of Ramadan on the Temple Mount
messages.ps: Despite the occupation's restrictions.. Palestinians make their way to Al-Aqsa on the first Friday of Ramadan
messages.neutral: Tens of thousands of worshippers attended prayers at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound on the first Friday of Ramadan.

messages.il:
IDF Spokesperson:

In the fighting at Shifa Hospital, the following were eliminated:

Fadi Dweik, released in the Shalit deal, with the blood of 4 Israelis on his hands, who continued to direct attacks in Judea and Samaria from his location in Gaza.

Zakariya Najib, released in the Shalit deal, a senior figure in the headquarters directing attacks in Judea and Samaria from his location in Gaza.

Mahmoud Zakzouk, deputy head of the rocket-fire array in Gaza City.

###
Those released in the Shalit deal stand at the heart of terrorist activity against Israel. Much Israeli blood is on their hands.
messages.ps:
⭕️ The Nazi occupation army claims:
The assassination of three released Shalit deal prisoners in the emergency department of al-Shifa Hospital in Gaza.
M
 #Ahrar_Tulkarm🔥🖤
messages.neutral: The Israeli military said it killed three Palestinians at al-Shifa Hospital in Gaza, including two former Shalit prisoner-exchange detainees.

messages.il: IDF Spokesperson: the IDF struck Hezbollah terror infrastructure in three areas in southern Lebanon. Fighter jets earlier today attacked terror infrastructure used by the Hezbollah terror organization in the areas of al-Taybeh, al-Naqoura, and Kafr Hanin.
messages.ps: ⭕️ The Israeli army: fighter jets bombed Hezbollah infrastructure in the areas of Taybeh, Naqoura, and Kafr Hanin in southern Lebanon. #Ahrar_Hizbullah🔥
messages.neutral: The Israeli military said its fighter jets struck Hezbollah infrastructure at sites in the al-Taybeh, al-Naqoura, and Kafr Hanin areas of southern Lebanon.

messages.il: This is happening in Qabatiya in the Jenin district over the last hour. Violent clashes between terrorists and our forces in the city.
messages.ps: Occupied Palestine: three young men wounded by occupation forces' bullets, one of them seriously, and the arrest of a citizen and his son during the raid into the town of Qabatiya south of Jenin
messages.neutral: Reports said Israeli forces entered Qabatiya, south of Jenin, and clashes occurred during the operation.

messages.il: The funeral now of the 13-year-old from Qabatiya who was eliminated this evening by the IDF in clashes in the city.
messages.ps: Scenes from the funeral of the martyr boy Mu'tasim Nabil Abu Abed in the town of Qabatiya south of Jenin.
messages.neutral: A funeral was held in Qabatiya, south of Jenin, for a 13-year-old boy who was killed earlier in the town.

messages.il: The official death toll in Lebanon has risen to 32. The actual death toll is much higher, including in the Radwan Force.
messages.ps: Urgent| Lebanese health minister: 32 martyrs in a new tally of the victims of communication-device explosions over the past two days.
messages.neutral: Lebanon’s health minister reported an updated official death toll of 32 from explosions involving communication devices over the past two days, while some reports suggested the true toll may be higher.

messages.il: Qabatiya, Jenin district: reports of several Palestinian dead in exchanges of fire with an IDF special force. The force is besieging one of the houses there. Sources identified with Fatah boast that two of those under siege are operatives in Fatah's "Al-Aqsa Martyrs' Brigades."
messages.ps: 5 martyrs in Qabatiya, Jenin district, in the occupied West Bank 🇵🇸
messages.neutral: Reports said five Palestinians were killed in Qabatiya, near Jenin.

messages.il: Qabatiya, Jenin district: official Palestinian media report 3 dead from IDF fire. Meanwhile the IDF bulldozer is working on the surrounded house...
messages.ps: Loudspeakers in the mosques of Qabatiya south of Jenin play Quran verses after at least 3 young men were martyred in a house that the occupation continues to besiege.
messages.neutral: Reports said at least three Palestinians were killed in Qabatiya, south of Jenin, during an Israeli operation in which a house was surrounded.

messages.il: Qabatiya, Jenin district: the Palestinians report casualties in a vehicle strike by an Israeli drone.
messages.ps: Red Crescent crews recover the body of a martyr from inside the vehicle targeted by the occupation in the town of Qabatiya south of Jenin.
messages.neutral: Reports said an Israeli drone struck a vehicle in Qabatiya, south of Jenin, killing one person.

messages.il: Nasrallah elaborates in praise of the decision Hezbollah made on 8.10 to serve as a supporting front for Gaza. He notes that it is a front that affects the enemy and serves as a bargaining chip in favor of the Palestinian resistance.
messages.ps: 🔴 Urgent | Sayyed Nasrallah: our front in support of Gaza was very effective and exerted very strong pressure on the enemy, and the proof is what it does and says, far from what is said here and there.
messages.neutral: Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah said the group's front opened in support of Gaza had been effective and increased pressure on Israel.

messages.il: Hezbollah claims responsibility for the firing toward Haifa earlier this morning: we launched Fadi 1 rockets at Kiryat Ata. Here again there is an admission by an organization that is part of the Lebanese government admits that it deliberately launches unguided weapons at a civilian population — a war crime. According to the IDF spokesperson, 10 rockets were launched; some were intercepted and some fell in open areas. Earlier, rockets fell inside the Sea of Galilee...
messages.ps: ⭕️ Hebrew sources: at least 10 rockets were launched from Lebanon toward Haifa in northern Palestine.
messages.neutral: Hezbollah said it fired rockets toward the Haifa area from Lebanon.

messages.il: IDF Spokesperson: "Arrows of the North" closing the loop from the air: the launcher from which rockets were fired this morning toward the Haifa and Krayot area was attacked. *Dozens of Hezbollah terror targets were attacked during the night and morning* Air Force fighter jets attacked a short while ago the launcher from which rockets were fired this morning toward the Haifa and Krayot area. In dozens of additional strikes, fighter jets and Air Force aircraft, directed by Northern Command, attacked military infrastructure, military buildings, and terrorist squads of the Hezbollah terror organization in many areas in southern Lebanon. The IDF continues to attack, strike, and degrade the capabilities and military infrastructure of the Hezbollah terror organization.
messages.ps: Hezbollah shells Haifa and the occupied Golan with rocket barrages.
messages.neutral: Reports said Hezbollah fired rocket salvos toward the Haifa area, and the Israeli military said it later struck the launcher used to fire the rockets and carried out additional airstrikes on targets in southern Lebanon.

messages.il: The barrage toward Tiberias: five launches fell in open areas; there are no reports of casualties.
messages.ps: Urgent | Hezbollah: for the second time, we shelled the occupied city of Tiberias with a rocket salvo.
messages.neutral: Hezbollah said it fired a rocket salvo toward Tiberias, and Israeli reports said no casualties were reported.

messages.il: The Houthis claim responsibility for launching a ballistic missile of the "Palestine 2" type toward Tel Aviv tonight. They also claim to have launched a drone of the "Yafa" type (Jaffa in Arabic—A.A.) toward a target in Ashkelon.
messages.ps: Urgent › The Yemeni armed forces: we targeted a site in the occupied Yafa area, "Tel Aviv," with a Palestine 2 ballistic missile
messages.neutral: The Houthis said they launched a 'Palestine 2' ballistic missile toward the Tel Aviv area overnight.

messages.il: Applause alongside boos at the UN: Netanyahu rises to speak | Live broadcast
messages.ps: The Palestinian representative and his delegation leave the United Nations hall as the speech of the occupation government's head "Netanyahu" begins
messages.neutral: As Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu began speaking at the United Nations, reactions in the hall were mixed and the Palestinian delegation left the chamber.

messages.il: The IDF Spokesperson announces that yesterday (Fri.), the IDF eliminated Hassan Nasrallah, leader of the Hezbollah terror organization and one of its founders, and together with him Ali Karaki, commander of Hezbollah's southern front, and additional Hezbollah commanders.
messages.ps: The mosques of Nablus mourn the martyred leader Hassan Nasrallah: "The assassination will only increase the party's determination for resistance and support of the Palestinian people"
messages.neutral: The Israeli military said it killed Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah in a strike, aand reports described public statements mourning his death in Nablus.

messages.il: Iranian sources identified with the regime: Brigadier General Abbas Nilforoushan, a senior member of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards (apparently the operations officer of the Quds Force), was eliminated in the strike in Dahieh yesterday.
messages.ps: Urgent| Iranian television: the martyrdom of Quds Force commander in Lebanon Abbas Nilforoushan in the attack on Beirut.
messages.neutral: Iranian media reported that Abbas Nilforoushan, described as a senior Quds Force commander, was killed in the attack on Beirut.

messages.il: Dozens of strikes in Dahieh in Beirut throughout the night. For many long hours the IDF continued to attack buildings in Dahieh. The Lebanese reported more than 40 strikes as of 03:00, and the strikes continued long afterward. At 03:00 the IDF spokesperson instructed the evacuation of three more buildings in Dahieh, which were then attacked. The Dahieh effect in all its glory. The entire Middle East is watching and understanding—we woke up to the dawn of a new day in the region.
messages.ps: Urgent| The occupation army: we specifically targeted Hezbollah's central command headquarters in Beirut's southern suburb.
messages.neutral: It was reported that Israel carried out dozens of airstrikes overnight in Beirut's southern suburbs, and the Israeli military said it targeted Hezbollah's central command headquarters in the area.

messages.il: The Shiite militias in Iraq claimed responsibility for launching a drone toward Tel Aviv.
messages.ps: Urgent| The Islamic Resistance in Iraq: this morning we attacked a vital target in "Tel Aviv" in our occupied Palestinian lands with drones.
messages.neutral: An Iraq-based armed group said it launched a drone attack targeting a site in Tel Aviv.

messages.il:
President Biden's response to the killing of Nasrallah:

Nasrallah and Hezbollah are responsible for the deaths of hundreds of Americans over four decades of terror. His death in an Israeli strike brings justice to his many victims: Americans, Israelis, and Lebanese.
Nasrallah tied his fate to Hamas a day after 7.10
The United States fully supports Israel's right to defend itself against Hezbollah, Hamas, the Houthis, and every other Iranian proxy.
I instructed the Secretary of Defense to strengthen the defense posture of the U.S. military in the Middle East in order to reduce the risk of a regional war.
messages.ps: Urgent| 🔻 Islamic Jihad Movement: we mourn to our dear Palestinian people, and to the Arab and Islamic nation, the martyrdom of Hezbollah Secretary-General His Eminence Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah, who was martyred after a hateful Zionist raid targeted him yesterday, Friday, at Hezbollah's central headquarters in Beirut's southern suburb. As we feel the pain and bitterness of parting, and the enormity of the loss of an Arab and Islamic resisting figure who charted the path of victory for the nation, we at the same time feel pride in his blessed legacy of tens of thousands of mujahideen, cadres, and leaders who were raised on his path, on the path of martyrdom and Palestine. We are fully confident that the martyrdom of Sayyed Nasrallah will increase the resistance in Lebanon, Palestine, and the region in strength, steadfastness, and resolve.
messages.neutral: After Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in an Israeli strike in Beirut, U.S. President Joe Biden said the attack brought justice and reaffirmed U.S. support for Israel's right to self-defense. Palestinian Islamic Jihad issued a statement mourning Nasrallah and praising his role in the regional armed movements.

messages.il: IDF Spokesperson: the IDF attacked and eliminated earlier today (Fri.) Mohammed Hamza Shahadi, intelligence officer in Hezbollah's "Radwan Force" in the Adloun area in southern Lebanon. During the war, the terrorist was engaged in force buildup and readiness for the "Radwan Force"; his actions constituted a violation of the understandings between Israel and Lebanon. The IDF will continue to act in order to remove any threat to the State of Israel.
messages.ps: Urgent | The occupation army announces the assassination of the resistance fighter "Mohammed Hamza Shahada," the intelligence official in Hezbollah's "Radwan Force," in a strike in the Adloun area in southern Lebanon. The occupation says the martyr had been working recently on building up the force and the operational readiness of the "Radwan Force."
messages.neutral: The Israeli military said it carried out a strike in the Adloun area of southern Lebanon that killed Mohammed Hamza Shahadi, whom it identified as an intelligence official in Hezbollah's Radwan Force.

messages.il: Gaza channels report more than ten wounded, some seriously, after an Israeli drone dropped grenades near the entrance gate of the As'ad al-Saftawi School in the Daraj neighborhood of Gaza City.
messages.ps: #Urgent | In the latest toll: more than 10 casualties have arrived at al-Ahli Hospital, including one extremely critical injury, as a result of an occupation drone dropping bombs on the gate of a school in the al-Daraj neighborhood of Gaza City
messages.neutral: Reports said more than 10 people were injured, including one critically, after an Israeli drone dropped explosives near the entrance gate of a school in Gaza City's al-Daraj neighborhood.

messages.il: The IDF Spokesperson claims responsibility for the killing of the "principal of al-Mansouri school" 👆 last night—he was a Hezbollah operative: last night (Sun.), the IDF, led by Division 91 and through the Air Force, attacked and eliminated the terrorist Mohammed Ali Shuayb from the Hezbollah terror organization in the al-Mansouri area in southern Lebanon. The terrorist served as a local representative of the Hezbollah terror organization in the village of al-Mansouri. As part of his role, he was responsible for the connection between the organization and the residents of the village on military and economic matters. He also worked to take over private assets for terror purposes.
messages.ps: The National Gathering of Public Sector Workers condemns the assassination of the director of al-Mansouri School and calls for schools to close in mourning
messages.neutral: The Israeli military said it carried out an airstrike in al-Mansouri in southern Lebanon that killed a man it identified as a Hezbollah operative. Local reports described the person killed as the director of al-Mansouri school.

messages.il: The UN Security Council adopted the American draft resolution to establish an international force in Gaza.
messages.ps:
🛑 Hamas Movement, in a press statement commenting on the UN Security Council's adoption of the American draft resolution regarding Gaza, affirms the following: this resolution does not rise to the level of our Palestinian people's political and humanitarian demands and rights, especially in the Gaza Strip, which has faced over two full years a brutal war of extermination and unprecedented crimes committed by the terrorist occupation before the eyes and ears of the world, and whose effects and repercussions remain ongoing despite the announcement of the end of the war according to President Trump's plan.
It imposes an international trusteeship mechanism on the Gaza Strip, which our people, their forces, and their factions reject. It also imposes a mechanism to achieve the goals of the occupation that it failed to achieve through the brutal war of extermination. This resolution also detaches the Gaza Strip from the rest of Palestinian geography and tries to impose new facts away from our people's constants and legitimate national rights, depriving our people of their right to self-determination and to establish their Palestinian state with Jerusalem as its capital.
messages.neutral: The UN Security Council adopted a U.S. drafted resolution on Gaza that proposed an international force or oversight mechanism, which Hamas said it opposed.

messages.il: Gaza channels report 4 dead, 9 wounded, and others missing in the strike on the building in the Sabra neighborhood of Gaza City 👆.
messages.ps: Urgent | 4 martyrs and dozens of missing and injured as a result of occupation aircraft destroying a house over the heads of its residents in the Sabra neighborhood, south of Gaza City.
messages.neutral: Reports said an airstrike hit a building in Gaza City's Sabra neighborhood, killing four people and leaving others injured or missing.

messages.il: People in Gaza report that the IDF set fire to the Israeli outpost in the al-Mughraqa area (Netzarim corridor) before withdrawing from the site as part of implementing the ceasefire agreement.
messages.ps: Urgent | Occupation soldiers are burning one of their sites while withdrawing from it in the al-Mughraqa area in the central Gaza Strip.
messages.neutral: Reports said Israeli forces set fire to a position they were withdrawing from in the al-Mughraqa area of central Gaza.

messages.il: Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas leader in the Strip and the person responsible for the negotiation file with Israel, in a speech marking the signing of the agreement: we received guarantees from the mediators and from the American administration that the war has ended completely. Al-Hayya praised the "blessed 7.10" and eulogized the senior figures who were eliminated, among them Sinwar, Haniyeh, and Deif.
messages.ps: Urgent | Hamas chief in Gaza, Khalil al-Hayya: we received guarantees from the mediators and from the American administration confirming that the war has ended completely.
messages.neutral: Khalil al-Hayya, a Hamas leader in Gaza, said that mediators and the U.S. administration had provided assurances that the war had ended.

messages.il: A little after one o'clock after midnight, the government approved the ceasefire agreement. IDF forces began withdrawing to the yellow line, which leaves about 53% of the Strip in Israel's hands—the withdrawal according to the agreement will last up to 24 hours from the moment the agreement was approved by the Israeli government. The Palestinians report the departure of the forces from the Nasr neighborhood in Gaza City, as well as from Jabalia camp. There is a limited return of Gazans to areas from which IDF forces withdrew.
messages.ps: Summary of events: after the session was postponed several times, and after direct American intervention in it, the occupation government approved late at night the ceasefire decision and announced that the occupation army would begin withdrawing after 24 hours. Despite the occupation government's approval, after which the agreement was supposed to enter into force, raids, shelling, and gunfire are still continuing in various areas of the Gaza Strip.
messages.neutral: Israeli authorities reported approving a ceasefire agreement shortly after midnight and said their forces would begin withdrawing within 24 hours, while Palestinian sources reported that attacks in parts of the Gaza Strip continued despite the approval.

messages.il: IDF Spokesperson: the ceasefire agreement entered into force as of 12:00. As of 12:00, IDF forces took up position along the updated deployment lines, in accordance with the outline of the ceasefire and hostage-return agreement. IDF forces under Southern Command are deployed in the area and will continue to act to remove any immediate threat.
messages.ps: Occupied Palestine: the Israeli occupation army: the ceasefire agreement in the Gaza Strip entered into force at 12:00 noon
messages.neutral: The Israeli military announced that a ceasefire agreement in the Gaza Strip took effect at 12:00.

messages.il: Reuters: the Red Cross has begun advancing toward the first hostage pickup point.
messages.ps: ⭕️ The Israeli army: Red Cross crews have begun moving toward the meeting point in the northern Gaza Strip, and the prisoners will be transferred there. #Ahrar_Tulkarm🔥🖤
messages.neutral: Reports said Red Cross teams began moving toward the first pickup point in northern Gaza.

messages.il: The Red Cross confirms it received 7 hostages.
messages.ps: ⭕️ The Red Cross announces that it has received 7 Israeli prisoners. #Ahrar_Tulkarm🔥🖤
messages.neutral: The International Committee of the Red Cross said it had received seven Israelis.

messages.il: President Trump arrives for a historic visit: "The war is over"
messages.ps: Trump: the war is over, the war is over, the war is over... and I believe the ceasefire will hold.
messages.neutral: U.S. President Donald Trump said the war had ended.

messages.il: Rafah: IDF fire toward a group of terrorists that approached. One of the terrorists was seriously wounded.
messages.ps: Urgent| A 16-year-old boy was injured by gunfire from occupation tanks near al-Alam junction south of the Mawasi area of Rafah, and he was transferred to the hospital.
messages.neutral: Israeli fire was reported near Rafah in southern Gaza, injuring one person.

messages.il: Trump clarifies in Sharm el-Sheikh that rebuilding the Strip is conditional on its demilitarization. From President Trump's remarks yesterday in Sharm el-Sheikh before the leaders participating in the conference: we all agreed that we do not want bloodshed, hatred, and terror, and therefore we agreed that rebuilding Gaza requires that it be demilitarized and that a new civilian police force create a new situation for the residents of the Strip.
messages.ps: Signing an agreement to end the war in Gaza during the Sharm el-Sheikh summit. The Egyptian city of Sharm el-Sheikh today witnessed the signing of a document ending the war on the Gaza Strip, during the peace summit held with the participation of leaders from the United States, Egypt, Turkey, and Qatar. It was signed by U.S. President Donald Trump, ...
messages.neutral: At a summit in Sharm el-Sheikh, a document to end the war in Gaza was signed by multiple participating parties, including U.S. President Donald Trump.

messages.il: President Trump explains in no uncertain terms that Hamas will be disarmed: Hamas will disarm because that is what it said. If they do not disarm, we will disarm them. I do not have to explain how that will happen. They know that I am not playing around. I spoke with Hamas and said to them: you will disarm, right? And they told me: yes sir, we will disarm. They will disarm or we will disarm them. It will happen quickly and perhaps also violently—but they will disarm. It will be interesting to hear Hamas's response to these remarks.
messages.ps: #Urgent Trump: Hamas's disarmament will happen quickly and perhaps violently.. the man is blunt..
messages.neutral: U.S. President Donald Trump said Hamas would be disarmed quickly, and indicated it could involve the use of force.

messages.il: U.S. President Donald Trump presented an outline for a political settlement centered on the establishment of a Palestinian state with territorial continuity "once they renounce terror." He announced that Jerusalem would remain Israel's undivided capital, but that in the future the Palestinian capital would be established in the eastern part of the city. Netanyahu: "A historic day like May 1948."
messages.ps: "Day of rage": Palestinians of all their organizational affiliations affirmed today their absolute rejection of Trump's plan for an alleged settlement of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, known as the "Deal of the Century," and hinted at withdrawing from the Oslo Accords and dissolving the Palestinian Authority.
messages.neutral: U.S. President Donald Trump unveiled a proposal for an Israeli-Palestinian political settlement that included a conditional Palestinian state.

messages.il: Netanyahu: "We will apply Israeli sovereignty to all the settlements." The prime minister launched the Likud campaign and promised in his speech to apply Israeli law to all settlement in Judea and Samaria "without exception and without a veto from Tibi." Senior officials in the Foreign Ministry clarify: this is a legislative proposal for election purposes only that will harm Israel in the international arena.
messages.ps: At the beginning of next July, the Israeli government intends to annex the Jordan Valley area and the settlements in the occupied West Bank to its sovereignty, something that observers see as possibly the final nail in the coffin of the political settlement process and the two-state option. Netanyahu indicated that "the annexation plans will not include Israeli government approval for the establishment of a Palestinian state." In response, Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas announced last month that the PLO is absolved of the agreements with Israel and the United States.
messages.neutral: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he would extend Israeli sovereignty to Israeli settlements in the West Bank, a proposal that drew criticism and warnings about its implications from Palestinian officials and others.

messages.il: History: Israel and the United Arab Emirates will establish full relations
messages.ps: The Palestinian leadership on Thursday affirmed its "rejection and condemnation" of the agreement to normalize relations between Israel and the UAE, brokered by the United States, considering it a "betrayal of Jerusalem" and calling for a meeting of the Arab League.
messages.neutral: Israel and the United Arab Emirates announced an agreement to establish full diplomatic relations.

messages.il: An attempt to calm the area: the Supreme Court accepted at noon (Sunday) the request of Attorney General Avichai Mandelblit and postponed the hearing on the appeal request concerning the evacuation of Arab residents from homes in the Sheikh Jarrah neighborhood in East Jerusalem.
messages.ps: The United Nations called on the Israeli occupation to exercise the utmost restraint in the Sheikh Jarrah neighborhood in occupied Jerusalem, against the background of the protests that broke out against the occupation's attempts to evict Jerusalemite families from their homes in favor of settlers.
messages.neutral: Israel's Supreme Court postponed a hearing related to planned evictions of Palestinian families from homes in Jerusalem's Sheikh Jarrah neighborhood amid heightened tensions and protests.

messages.il: A night of clashes in Jerusalem: thousands of Palestinians rioted, dozens of police officers went up to the Temple Mount
messages.ps: An Al Jazeera correspondent reported that calm returned to the courtyards of Al-Aqsa Mosque and that the occupation forces withdrew outside it after storming it, besieging thousands of worshippers there, and trying to empty the prayer halls by force, resulting in 205 worshippers being injured. The Palestinian presidency called for international protection, while the factions vowed to respond.
messages.neutral: Clashes erupted at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem after Israeli police entered the site.

messages.il: A special civil emergency was declared in Lod, Border Police forces entered the city. After Jerusalem and Gaza, the severe violence also spread to cities where Jews and Arabs live side by side. Border Police forces were transferred from the West Bank to Lod and other mixed cities.
messages.ps: Palestinian sources in the city of Lod ("al-Lydd") in central 1948-occupied Palestine revealed the identity of the Palestinian young man who was martyred late last night after being shot by a settler belonging to the Torah nucleus in the city of Lod. The shooting came from a settler minutes after a tweet by the extremist Knesset member Bezalel Smotrich, in which he called on Jews "to defend themselves against the enemy and shoot to defend themselves," in his words.
messages.neutral: Amid escalating unrest and violence in Lod, Israeli authorities deployed Border Police forces to the city.

messages.il: Terror raises its head in Jerusalem's Old City: a civilian about 30 years old was murdered this morning (Sunday) and two others were seriously and moderately wounded in a shooting attack in the Chain Gate area near the Temple Mount in the capital. Two Border Police fighters were lightly wounded. The terrorist Fadi Abu Shkhaydam (42), a member of Hamas's political wing from Shuafat refugee camp, was shot dead by security forces. An initial investigation found that the shooting was carried out with a standard rifle. A knife was found beside the terrorist's body.
messages.ps: Occupied Jerusalem—gunfire rang out, accompanied by the screams of dozens of occupation soldiers, then ambulance crews converged on the road to Bab al-Silsila, just a few meters from the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque. Speculation began, and the certain news came after half an hour: the attacker was martyred, and one Israeli soldier was killed and 3 others wounded, one of them critically.
messages.neutral: A shooting attack near the Chain Gate in Jerusalem's Old City left one Israeli dead and several others injured, and the attacker was shot and killed by Israeli security forces.

messages.il: At the end of 48 hours of focused operational activity, Operation "House and Garden" came to an end—and in the security system they are celebrating a significant achievement. "Any terrorist who returns to the refugee camp in Jenin will not recognize it; for him it is no longer a safe place," the minister clarified.
messages.ps: The "Jenin Battalion" affiliated with "Saraya al-Quds" (the military wing of Islamic Jihad) launched at dawn today, Monday, the operation "Might of Jenin" in response to the occupation army's aggression against the city and its camp.
messages.neutral: Israeli forces carried out a roughly two-day military operation in Jenin and its refugee camp, during which clashes occurred with Palestinian armed groups.

messages.il: About 50,000 demonstrators are clashing with IDF forces along the Gaza border, throwing explosive devices, Molotov cocktails, and stones. The Palestinians: dozens were killed and hundreds wounded. The IDF: 3 terrorists who tried to place an explosive device were eliminated, the Air Force struck Hamas positions.
messages.ps: 37 Palestinians, including two children, were martyred and more than a thousand wounded by occupation fire as thousands of demonstrators flocked to the return camps on the eastern border of the Gaza Strip. In the West Bank, the March of Return was launched to commemorate the Nakba and reject the transfer of the U.S. embassy to Jerusalem, which was surrounded by tight occupation security measures. The Palestinian leadership also called for an emergency meeting.
messages.neutral: Large protests took place along the Gaza border amid clashes with Israeli forces, with Palestinian officials reporting dozens killed and many injured, and Israel reporting that it targeted several individuals and attacked Hamas positions.

messages.il: A historic step to advance the international standing of Israel's capital: 800 statesmen, lawmakers, diplomats, rabbis, and priests participated in the official opening ceremony of the senior American mission. Trump, in a filmed greeting: Israel is a sovereign nation with the right, like any other sovereign nation, to determine its capital. Netanyahu: a day that will be etched in our national memory for generations
messages.ps: The American embassy officially in Jerusalem.. Israeli jubilation and Palestinian anger: since the morning, thousands of Palestinians have demonstrated at several points along the border fence separating eastern Gaza and Israel, in protest of the embassy transfer and to mark the 70th anniversary of the Nakba. According to the Palestinian Health Ministry, the demonstrations resulted in dozens of martyrs.
messages.neutral: The United States formally opened its embassy in Jerusalem.

messages.il: The 17-year-old Palestinian girl, who was documented hitting an IDF officer and soldier, was released from Hasharon Prison and transferred together with her mother, who also completed her prison term, to the Rantis checkpoint, where they were released to their home. Ahead of their arrival: a Palestinian flag was hung on their house in Nabi Saleh
messages.ps: Ahed Tamimi and her mother to freedom. The Israeli occupation authorities released this morning, Sunday, the Palestinian girl Ahed Tamimi and her mother Nariman after they completed their eight-month prison sentence.
messages.neutral: Palestinian Ahed Tamimi and her mother, Nariman Tamimi, were released from Israeli custody after completing their prison sentences.

messages.il: Messi will not come, in a major victory for the BDS movement: following pro-Palestinian demonstrations and threats, the Argentinians decided to cancel the friendly match. Report: "Messi and the coach pushed for the decision." Netanyahu spoke with the president of Argentina, Regev: "I hope they will not surrender to threats of terror." Ticket buyers will get their money back
messages.ps: Nazareth - Gaza - Al-Quds Al-Arabi, by Wadie Awawdeh and Ashraf al-Hour: yesterday the Palestinians scored a "political, diplomatic, and sporting goal into the Israeli net" after their great efforts succeeded in convincing the Argentine national football team, led by the famous player Lionel Messi, to cancel its friendly match with the Israeli team, which was scheduled to be held the day after tomorrow, Saturday, in a stadium built on the ruins of the Palestinian village of al-Malha in occupied Jerusalem. While Palestinians expressed their joy and thanked Argentina and its team for the wise decision, which reflects commitment to international resolutions, bewilderment, shock, and anger spread through the Israeli street despite the official bodies' silence.
messages.neutral: Argentina's national football team cancelled a planned friendly match against Israel.

messages.il: The legal saga over the takeover by lawbreaking Bedouins of state lands in the Ma'ale Adumim plain has lasted ten years. About six months ago, the High Court of Justice already rejected the Bedouins' petition and that of their Israeli helpers against the state's decision to carry out the demolition orders. But left-wing organizations pushed the Bedouins to petition again. It is hard to understand why the High Court did not treat its previous ruling as final and conclusive, but it heard the request again, and on September 5, 2018, the High Court finally rejected the request and ruled that the state is permitted to demolish.
messages.ps: The Israeli court's decision to evacuate and demolish the Khan al-Ahmar community east of Jerusalem sparked a wave of denunciation and condemnation on many levels and in many circles—Palestinian, Arab, and international. Those condemning it warned of the danger of this step, considering it a flagrant violation of international law and the decisions of the international community. The Grand Mufti of Jerusalem and the Palestinian territories, preacher of Al-Aqsa Mosque Sheikh Muhammad Hussein, denounced the Israeli court's decision to demolish and evacuate the community and described it as a racist decision.
messages.neutral: Israel's Supreme Court ruled that the government could proceed with the evacuation and demolition of the Khan al-Ahmar Bedouin community east of Jerusalem, a decision that drew condemnation and legal objections from Palestinian officials.

messages.il: In Israel they are responding for the first time to the ceasefire: according to a senior political source, Hamas "suffered a hard blow and asked for a ceasefire"—and agreed to stop the fire terror. On the other hand, Hamas claims: nothing was agreed regarding the arson. A fire broke out in the south as a result of an incendiary balloon
messages.ps: After an Israeli escalation and the resistance's response.. a return to calm in Gaza. The Islamic Resistance Movement (Hamas) confirmed the start of a new truce tonight in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli escalation that caused four martyrs, while the Palestinian resistance's responses resulted in the death of an Israeli soldier.
messages.neutral: A ceasefire was reported to have taken effect in Gaza after an escalation between Israel and Hamas, with the two sides giving differing accounts of the terms.

messages.il: "I am here to announce my resignation from the position of Israel's defense minister," Lieberman began his remarks. "The question is why now? From my perspective, what happened yesterday—the ceasefire together with the entire settlement process with Hamas—is surrender to terror. It has no other meaning but surrender to terror. What we are actually doing now is buying short-term quiet at the price of severe damage to national security in the long term."
messages.ps: Israeli Defense Minister Avigdor Lieberman submitted his resignation a day after the announcement of a ceasefire between Israel and the resistance factions in the Gaza Strip, and the Palestinian factions considered this resignation an admission of defeat. Lieberman said in a press conference that his decision came against the background of the government's approval of the ceasefire agreement, which he described as "surrender to terror," and because of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's decision to allow 15 million dollars from the State of Qatar to enter Gaza, claiming that it was for the benefit of Hamas.
messages.neutral: Israeli Defense Minister Avigdor Lieberman resigned following the announcement of a ceasefire with Gaza-based armed groups, saying he opposed the agreement and related measures involving Qatar's funding for Gaza.

messages.il: "Happy to be back, we will produce an amazing Eurovision in Tel Aviv." Jon Ola Sand, the international supervisor of Eurovision, raised expectations at a festive opening event held ahead of the 2019 competition. Huldai surprised people and played the Eurovision theme on a flute
messages.ps: Hundreds of artists sign a petition rejecting "Eurovision 2019": holding the artistic festival in (Israel) created a real crisis for hundreds of artists because of what (Tel Aviv) is doing in polishing Israeli colonialism and whitewashing its crimes against the Palestinians through art, just as through sport. The contest is being held at the same time as Palestinians mark the 71st anniversary of the Palestinian Nakba, while the Israeli occupation continues the siege of Gaza, the strangling of the West Bank, and the seizure of its lands for settlement projects.
messages.neutral: Ahead of the Eurovision Song Contest planned for Tel Aviv in 2019, organizers promoted the event while some artists and activists called for a boycott in protest of Israel's policies toward Palestinians.

messages.il: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu met today (Monday) with U.S. President Donald Trump. In the meeting at the White House, Trump signed a presidential order recognizing Israeli sovereignty over the Golan Heights. Netanyahu praised Trump and compared him to foreign leaders in history—such as Cyrus, Harry Truman, and Lord Balfour.
messages.ps: The countries of the world, the United Nations, and the European Union rushed to reject U.S. President Donald Trump's decision today to formally recognize Israel's "sovereignty" over the occupied Syrian Golan Heights.
messages.neutral: During a White House meeting with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, U.S. President Donald Trump signed a proclamation recognizing Israeli sovereignty over the Golan Heights.

messages.il: Almost final: a sweeping victory for the right-wing bloc, awaiting the counting of the last three regular polling stations
messages.ps: Abbas hopes that the results of the Israeli elections will bring peace. Ashrawi: "These elections are a competition over who can suppress the Palestinian people more, steal more of its land, build settlements on it, and pledge to annex Jerusalem, and therefore who can more completely destroy the chances of peace altogether"
messages.neutral: Preliminary results in Israel's election indicated a strong showing for the right-wing bloc as final ballots were still being counted, prompting Palestinian leaders to comment on the implications for the peace process.

messages.il: A ceasefire with Hamas; Sa'ar: "The campaign was not prevented, but postponed"
messages.ps: 31 martyrs and 830 damaged housing units.. Gaza counts its losses after the aggression stopped
messages.neutral: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took effect after fighting in Gaza, as Gaza officials reported dozens of deaths and widespread damage to housing.

messages.il: The Bahrain conference concluded, Kushner optimistic: the plan is feasible, we are enthusiastic. At the end of the "economic workshop" initiated by the White House, Trump's senior adviser responded to the criticism and said he was impressed by the conference's achievements. "People did not know what to expect. They are leaving energized and enthusiastic." Kushner admitted that it would be harder to find a solution to the political aspects. "We left the door open for the Palestinians"
messages.ps: The "Manama workshop" promises Palestinians "investment" and ignores rights. The final statement of the economic workshop, which lasted two days in Bahrain, did not mention the conditions of the Palestinians under Israeli occupation.
messages.neutral: A U.S.-backed economic conference in Bahrain concluded after presenting proposals linked to the Trump administration's peace initiative, which U.S. officials promoted as feasible and Palestinian leaders criticized as overlooking core political issues.
